last_cycle_summary: Added a reusable local-first Aletheia Kernel facade over the existing V1 AgentOps contracts. The new kernel module names the core modules, exposes the default least-privilege tool policy, binds Private Contract / Due Diligence Review as a domain pack, fails final export closed unless gates and explicit export permission pass, blocks external model/tool capabilities by default, and keeps skill activation dependent on approved skill status plus approved playbook provenance.
tests_run: cd frontend && ../backend/node_modules/.bin/tsx --test tests/agentops/kernel.test.ts tests/agentops/v1Runtime.test.ts tests/agentops/exportPackage.test.ts tests/agentops/skillsEval.test.ts
next_actions: Wire kernel export decisions into the Remote Matter final export UI/API boundary so the facade becomes the single final-export authorization path.
Add a small kernel architecture doc that points public docs from Kernel to Domain Packs without duplicating V1 implementation details.
Consider moving existing V1 runtime policy checks to consume evaluateKernelModelPolicy directly.
